name: Louisiana Fair Housing Action Center, INC.
description_short: Louisiana Fair Housing Action Center provides free fair housing counseling, discrimination complaint assistance, and eviction defense to residents facing housing discrimination or displacement.
description_long:
The Louisiana Fair Housing Action Center is a 30-year-old nonprofit organization dedicated to combating housing discrimination and segregation in Louisiana, with a focus on the New Orleans area. The organization works to address systemic housing issues stemming from historical practices like redlining and exclusionary policies that continue to affect protected classes and vulnerable communities. Their mission explicitly centers on ensuring fair housing access and preventing discrimination in rental, purchase, and lending decisions.

The organization offers multiple free services to the public, including filing discrimination complaints, eviction defense attorney consultations, housing counselor services, and fair housing testing programs. They provide direct legal assistance through eviction defense, community education on fair housing rights, and advocacy work to influence local and state policy. Additionally, they engage in public education campaigns around elections and affordable housing bond measures, positioning fair housing as a civic priority.

The Louisiana Fair Housing Action Center distinguishes itself through its deep community roots and policy advocacy work. Unlike basic housing counseling services, they combine individual complaint resolution with systemic advocacy, including direct outreach to political candidates about fair housing platforms. Their 30-year track record in the state and focus on housing segregation as a structural issue—not just individual discrimination—sets them apart. They also operate a fair housing tester program, which involves trained individuals who test for discriminatory practices.

However, the website provides limited detail about specific counseling methodologies, success rates, or geographic service areas beyond general Louisiana/New Orleans references. The organization appears primarily focused on advocacy and complaint-filing rather than comprehensive housing counseling or credit-related financial assistance. Potential clients should contact directly to confirm availability and specific service details.
pros: Completely free services—no fees for discrimination complaints, eviction defense, or housing counseling
30 years of organizational history and expertise in fair housing enforcement
Direct legal assistance through eviction defense attorney consultations
Addresses systemic housing issues including redlining legacy and segregation patterns
Maintains fair housing tester program to identify discriminatory practices
Engages in policy advocacy with local government candidates and officials
Free complaint filing process for housing discrimination allegations
cons: Website does not specify geographic service area or whether services are available statewide or only in New Orleans
No information on counselor credentials, certifications (HUD-approval not mentioned), or counseling methodologies
No data provided on complaint resolution rates, average processing times, or success metrics
Limited details on specific counseling topics covered (budgeting, credit repair, lease negotiation)
Website does not clearly explain eligibility criteria or how to schedule services
best_for: Renters facing housing discrimination based on protected class status (race, family status, disability, etc.)
Tenants facing eviction who need legal representation and defense strategies
Individuals experiencing housing segregation or redlining patterns in Louisiana
diagnosis: The Louisiana Fair Housing Action Center is best for renters and homebuyers in Louisiana facing housing discrimination complaints or eviction, seeking free legal and counseling support. The main caveat is that this organization focuses on fair housing rights and discrimination remediation rather than credit counseling or personal finance management, so it is not suitable for general financial or credit-building needs.
services: Community education on fair housing rights
Policy advocacy and outreach to elected officials
Affordable housing advocacy and bond measure education
Information on housing segregation and discriminatory practices
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
